title: Object Scorecard Limit with Upgrade Prompt
story: As a product administrator, I want a limit on the number of Salesforce objects in my scorecard with a clear upgrade prompt when I reach the cap, so that free-tier usage is bounded and users are guided toward paid plans for expanded capacity.
description:
Enforces a limit on the number of Salesforce objects a user can add to their Data Dictionary scorecard. When a user attempts to add an object beyond the limit, a popup notifies them they have reached the cap and offers an upgrade path.

The limit is set to 15 objects (title mentions 15, description mentions 10 — implemented as 15). Clicking 'OK' on the limit popup opens a slide-out panel with subscription/upgrade options.
acceptance_criteria: Users can add up to 15 objects to their scorecard
Attempting to add a 16th object triggers a popup notification about the limit
Clicking 'OK' on the popup opens an upgrade/subscription slide-out panel
Clicking 'Cancel' dismisses the popup without action
Existing users with more than 15 objects are not affected (grandfathered)
ux_changes: Popup modal appears when the user exceeds the scorecard object limit. 'OK' opens an upgrade slide-out panel. 'Cancel' dismisses the prompt.
middleware_changes: Server-side validation checks the object count against the plan limit before adding a new object. Returns a limit-exceeded response if the cap is reached.
implementation_notes: The limit discrepancy between summary (15) and description (10) was resolved to 15 in implementation. The upgrade flow uses a slide-out panel consistent with other subscription prompts in the application.
comment_summary: Developer implemented the 15-object limit with popup and slide-out upgrade panel. Deployed to development for review.
